# The Highland building packet

## Snapshot
- Building ID: the-highland-1411-n-highland-ave
- Address: 1411 N Highland Ave, Hollywood, CA 90028
- Primary URL: https://www.thehighlandgk.com/
- Management company: Greystar California, Inc. dba Greystar Corp.
- Recommended status: tour_candidate
- Last updated: 2026-03-30

## Scorecard
- Quiet: 3 (medium, evidence count 1)
- Management: 4 (medium, evidence count 2)
- Amenity: 5 (high, evidence count 3)
- Community stability: 3 (medium, evidence count 1)
- Location fit: 4
- Pricing transparency: 4

## Risks
- Structural noise risk: unknown
- Location noise risk: high
- Security risk: low
- Pricing risk: low

## Evidence summary
- [official] The official site markets a fitness center, rooftop pool deck, in-unit washer and dryer, business center, conference room, and EV charging. Source: The Highland official site (https://www.thehighlandgk.com/). Note: Official materials describe a rooftop pool deck, fitness center, washer and dryer, business center, conference room, and EV charging.
- [review] Apartments.com publishes an unusually clear fee table, including $35 application fee, $1,500 security deposit, $65 monthly pet rent, and $120 monthly parking. Source: Apartments.com (https://www.apartments.com/the-highland-hollywood-ca/0pwyef2/). Note: The Fees and Policies section lists a $35 application fee, $1,500 security deposit, $65 pet rent, and $120 parking fee, while noting that displayed prices are total monthly price.
- [review] Apartments.com corroborates package service, controlled access, video patrol, key-fob entry, conference rooms, business center, in-unit washer and dryer, dishwasher, and AC. Source: Apartments.com (https://www.apartments.com/the-highland-hollywood-ca/0pwyef2/). Note: Property Services and Apartment Features list package service, controlled access, video patrol, key fob entry, business center, conference rooms, washer/dryer, air conditioning, and dishwasher.
- [review] Apartments.com shows a strong 4.7 blended score across 21 renter reviews. Source: Apartments.com (https://www.apartments.com/the-highland-hollywood-ca/0pwyef2/). Note: The public profile shows 4.7 out of 5 with 21 reviews.
- [review] Aggregator inventory was inconsistent on 2026-03-30, with Apartments.com and Zillow showing different active unit numbers for the same property. Source: Zillow (https://www.zillow.com/apartments/hollywood-ca/the-highland/CkMJKN/). Note: Zillow showed units 514, 510, and 424 as active while Apartments.com showed units 210 and 302, so leasing should confirm the true live list.
- [review] The building sits in a high-activity pocket near Hollywood/Highland station and Sunset/La Brea retail, which makes unit placement central to quiet. Source: Apartments.com (https://www.apartments.com/the-highland-hollywood-ca/0pwyef2/). Note: Apartments.com places the property 0.3 miles from Hollywood/Highland Station and within 0.5 miles of multiple shopping centers around Sunset and La Brea.
- [official] Greystar is clearly disclosed as the operating party, which improves accountability compared with buildings where management is not surfaced at all. Source: The Highland official site (https://www.thehighlandgk.com/). Note: Official copy identifies Greystar California, Inc. dba Greystar Corp. on the property profile.
- [review] The Highland's amenity profile includes planned social activity spaces, rooftop lounge areas, and on-site retail, which suggests a moderately active shared environment rather than a purely quiet one. Source: Apartments.com (https://www.apartments.com/the-highland-hollywood-ca/0pwyef2/). Note: The public profile lists planned social activities, roof terrace, lounge space, and on-site retail.

## Open questions
- Confirm whether the target unit is set back from Highland and Hollywood activity and whether rooftop or pool-deck noise carries into the stack.
- Ask leasing to reconcile current live inventory, because Apartments.com and Zillow showed different active unit numbers on 2026-03-30.
- Get the exact lease math in writing: concession applicability, garage pricing, utilities, and any mandatory monthly charges beyond the displayed total monthly price.

## Notes
The Highland is one of the better paper fits in this batch. Public materials support package service, controlled access, video patrol, EV charging, business-center and conference-room space, in-unit laundry, dishwasher, AC, and a transparent fee table. The tradeoff is location intensity, not missing essentials. It sits in an active Hollywood pocket near Highland, Hollywood/Highland station, and Sunset/La Brea retail, so quiet depends heavily on stack selection. Management signal is good but not perfect: Apartments.com shows 4.7 out of 5 across 21 reviews, which is enough to advance it to a tour candidate but not enough to skip in-person verification.
